rationale: PR adds an OpenHands-based good-first-issue classifier with the conservative coupling to duplicate-detection the body describes. .github/workflows/issue-duplicate-checker.yml gains a 'good-first-issue-check' workflow_dispatch mode plus a new issue-good-first-issue-check job that depends on issue-duplicate-check via `needs:` and reads its new outputs (should_comment, is_duplicate, auto_close_candidate, confidence, classification). The job's precheck step skips auto-labeling when the issue already carries 'good first issue', already has 'duplicate-candidate', or duplicate-check returned duplicate/overlapping-scope (using DUPLICATE_SHOULD_COMMENT and DUPLICATE_CLASSIFICATION env vars). The new scripts/issue_good_first_issue_check_openhands.py uses urllib (stdlib, no new deps) to call OpenHands Cloud /api/v1/app-conversations with a structured prompt requiring all 5 explicit newcomer criteria; the script enforces should_apply_label=false unless confidence is high. ISSUE_TRIAGE.md is updated to describe the new flow and conservative rules. A new tests/unit/test_issue_good_first_issue_check_openhands.py provides focused unit coverage. Error handling preserves causes via `raise RuntimeError(...) from exc` matching the project's preserve-caught-error policy. Real implementation, honest scope, no cheats.
